data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T5c — Security overlay). The overlay APPLIES: this plan stands up real, internet-reachable security surface — a public HTTPS review-bot endpoint (`/review/` on 443, nginx→127.0.0.1:${REVIEW_BOT_PORT}) plus Gerrit HTTP 8080 / SSH 29418, an authn boundary on inbound webhooks, and multiple credentials/grants (a GitHub replication deploy key, a Gerrit bot HTTP token, an EC2 instance role reading SSM SecureStrings + KMS decrypt). Verdict: sound on 3 of the 4 sub-checks (access/authz, least-privilege, secret-source); 2 grounded coaching findings, both on data-at-rest / secret-in-logs. I re-verified both against the live infra/ code — they still hold.

AFFIRMATIONS (T5c criteria this plan satisfies):
- (a) Endpoint access levels declared + sensitive paths gated: `/review/` on 443 is the only public surface (nginx/rebar.conf.template:54-60); the stdio MCP is deliberately NOT exposed over HTTP. Inbound webhook auth is a two-layer control and honestly scoped — PRIMARY is internal-only container-to-container delivery over the private compose network (webhooks.config:13-19: `http://review-bot:8000/webhook`, receiver host-port loopback-bound `127.0.0.1:8000`, port 8000 NOT in the SG, so unreachable except via nginx `/review/`); SECONDARY is the URL-embedded token with receiver-side token/source validation. The `webhooks`-plugin "NO HMAC" limitation is NAMED (webhooks.config:12) rather than papered over. IMDSv2 required (main.tf:50-54) defends the instance-role creds against SSRF theft. Per-(change_id, revision) single-flight + idempotency guard the review/vote critical section against retries/backfill.
- (c) LEAST-PRIVILEGE on new grants: strong. Replication deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only to GitHub and the sole ruleset bypass actor; the bot HTTP token's dual scope (REST `/a/` + git-over-HTTPS read) is documented + single-sourced; SSM read scoped to the `/rebar/prod/*` prefix (explicitly NOT Resource `*`) with kms:Decrypt via a kms:ViaService condition; the IAM-role ownership contract (S1 CREATES the role; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies) prevents over-grant/clobber. No wildcard `*:*` / admin-for-convenience.
- (d) Secret lifecycle (largely): SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; enumerated SSM param set completeness-checked (`/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` + `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`); no plaintext secret committed to IaC (webhooks.config:23 uses a `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder substituted at boot). The two findings below keep this from a clean (d).

FINDINGS (2):

- [ ] Specify encryption-at-rest (`encrypted = true` + KMS) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS data volume AND the root device, so it and its inherited DLM snapshots are encrypted at rest. AC1 / the "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet name only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume ... with prevent_destroy + DLM daily snapshots" with NO at-rest-encryption statement. This volume holds `refs/meta/config`, where per ADR-0014 the live webhook bot token is stored, and DLM snapshots INHERIT the source volume's (currently absent) encryption. Code confirms the gap: `aws_ebs_volume "data"` (infra/terraform/main.tf:27-40) sets `type = "gp3"` + `prevent_destroy` but declares NO `encrypted`; `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) likewise; and a repo-wide `search infra/ "encrypted"` returns ZERO matches (so even the root device is unencrypted, contra derisk note E1's "gp3-encrypted" claim, which dry-ran only the root-device shape).
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions → "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-59, infra/terraform/backup.tf
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; code fact — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) and `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) declare no `encrypted`, and `search infra/ "encrypted"` → 0 hits; ADR-0014: the bot token lives in `refs/meta/config`, i.e. on this volume; T5c(b) at-rest encryption where sensitive data is stored.
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed EBS key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data` and `root_block_device`; add a line to S1's volume AC stating the encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest.

- [ ] Address the token-in-query-string log-leak vector: state a redaction posture for `?token=`. The bot token travels as a `?token=<token>` query parameter on every webhook POST (infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23: `url = http://review-bot:8000/webhook?token=__BOT_TOKEN__`) AND on the PUBLIC operator `/review/rerun?token=$TOKEN&change=...` path over `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/...` (infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:34). Query strings are a classic secret-in-logs sink (nginx access log, receiver request log, and — for the webhook hop — Gerrit's webhooks-plugin delivery log). The plan/ADR-0014 bound the token's exposure in `refs/meta/config` but do NOT state that `?token=` is redacted on the log path — and the public nginx `/review/` location (infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template:54-60) sets no `access_log off`/redaction, while `search infra/ "access_log"` returns ZERO matches (no log config pinned), so the default combined log records the full URI including the token for the `/rerun` path.
  - criteria: [T5c]
  - location: Round-11 remediation → inbound-auth note ("inbound auth is a secret-token URL"); infra/gerrit/webhooks.config:23; infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md:34; infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template:54-60
  - evidence: code fact — the token is in the URL query on both the webhook and the PUBLIC `/rerun` path; T5c(d) "no secret-logging"; ABSENCE of any log-redaction statement for `?token=` across the nginx/receiver/webhooks-plugin log path (`search infra/ "access_log"` → 0 hits).
  - suggested_fix: State the redaction posture in S4a — e.g. nginx `/review/` logs a redacted URI (map the request URI to strip `token=…`, or `access_log off` on that location) and the receiver masks the token in its request log; OR move the token to an HTTP header if the plugin/receiver supports one, keeping only the network-ACL primary gate in the URL.
